name: substr_cpy
title: substr_cpy
syntax: void substr_cpy(char dest[], char src[], long srcStart, long len, long max);
description: This function copies a substring of src to dest. max indicates the size of dest in bytes. The function ensures that there is a terminating '\0'. Thus, a maximum of max-1 bytes are copied.

A character may need several bytes depending on the string encoding, e.g. Japanese characters in Windows ANSI (932) encoding or any special characters in UTF-8 encoding. In that case, you should use mbsubstr_cpy instead.
